body_txt: After a character gains and then loses a Love Interest , any future relationships become hard or even impossible to develop and maintain. Usually, the reason this happens is because the results of the character's last relationship have brought upon them such an emotional baggage that trying to pursue a Second Love turns into an exercise in pain and frustration. For example, they might be reminded of their failed relationship which ends up straining their new one; their feelings for the person they loved might be strong enough that it interferes with how they treat later romantic partners; they may have tragically lost their first partner and, fearing the same happening again, are avoiding future relationships. Alternatively, the character may not so much be hurt as they are unwilling to give romantic relationships a second chance when the first one doesn't work out. They may also be angry and untrusting if their relationship ended because their partner did something horrible, such as hurting or betraying them in some way, which gave them the impression that relationships will only lead to bad outcomes and thus don't see it worth risking it again. If the emotional baggage is not worked out, the failed relationship may become an Anchored Ship . Compare The Mourning After , if the ending of the relationship was particularly traumatic. Definitely an example of Love Hurts .
